questions.explanation: Watch is showing time with an interval of 2 hours. The last watch shows 8 o’clock, so, the next will show 10 o’clock.

questions.question: Three statements are given, followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: No bench is chair. All sofas are benches. All tables are sofas. Conclusions: I. No table is a chair. II. Some sofas are chairs. III. All tables are benches.
options: Only conclusions I and II follow
Only conclusions I and III follow
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997042eb3c5c9.59465704.png) Only I and III follow

questions.explanation: Only sweet - 55 Only spicy - 65 Total = 55 + 65 = 120

questions.question: 7 is related to 40 by certain logic. Following the same logic, 5 is related to 16. To which of the following is 9 related, following the same logic? [NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /deleting /multiplying etc., to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.]

Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. [The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/number of consonants/vowels in the word]? Temerity : Shyness :: Pique : ?
questions.explanation: Shyness is the antonym of temerity. In the same way, delight is the antonym of pique.

questions.question: Six letters A, B, C, E, F and G are written on different faces of a dice. Two positions of this dice are shown in the figure below. Find the letter on the face opposite to E. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970434058dd5.38243962.png)
questions.explanation: A is common in both the dice with B,C and G,F. Hence, the only letter is remaining is E which is opposite to A.

questions.question: Rikhampada dance belongs to which of the following states?
questions.explanation: Rikhampada is a dance of Nishi tribe of lower Subansiri District of Arunachal Pradesh. It is believed that this dance and the Nyokum festival were introduced by one of their forefathers named Apatani, when he along with his tribe migrated to the present place of settlement. The dance is accompanied by songs in the form of ballads narrating legends of love. It is performed by women only to express their love for their husbands and to offer their reverence to the gods.

questions.question: Which of the following Harappan sites is in Haryana?
questions.explanation: Rakhigarhi, situated on the banks of ancient Saraswati River in Hisar district of Haryana, was discovered by ASI team in 1960. This is the largest city of the Harappan civilization. Abrasive stones for smoothing beads, elephant teeth, swamp deer horns, bead making workshop etc. have been found from this site.

questions.question: Who among the following introduced the Daag and Huliya system and cash payment system for soldiers in the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: The Daag and Huliya system, as well as the cash payment system for soldiers in the Delhi Sultanate, were introduced by Alauddin Khalji during his reign

questions.question: As a reaction to Rowlatt Act, ––––– was organised as National Humiliation Day?
questions.explanation: In March, 1919 the Rowlatt Act was passed which was based on the report of the Rowlatt commission. This gave law power to the police to arrest any person without any reason. Gandhi who had formed a Satyagraha Sabha earlier called for a country wide protest. Throughout the country 6 April, 1919 was observed as a National Humiliation Day

questions.question: Which of the following Sufi order was the most orthodox?
questions.explanation: The Naqshbandi silsilah was founded by Khwaja Bahauddin Naqshbandi. It was the most conservative among Sufi sects. He opposed the liberal policies of Akbar while the Chishti sect is the oldest Sufi chain of India. It was founded by Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti in the 12th century. Qadri order was established in India by Shah Nayamatullan Qadiri and was introduced in India over Babur period.

questions.question: The northern part of which of the following is also known as sahyadri?? > निम्नलिखित में से किसके उत्तरी भाग को सह्याद्रि के नाम से भी जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Northern part of the Western Ghat is also known as sahyadri. The range is called sahayadri in northern Maharashtra and Sahya Parvatam in Kerala. The northern portion of the narrow coastal plain between the western ghats and the Arabian sea is known as the Konkan coast. The central portion is called kanara and the southern portion is called Malabar region of the Malabar Coast. > पश्चिमी घाट के उत्तरी भाग को सह्याद्रि के नाम से भी जाना जाता है। इस श्रेणी को उत्तरी महाराष्ट्र में सहयाद्रि और केरल में सह्या पर्वतम कहा जाता है। पश्चिमी घाट और अरब सागर के बीच संकीर्ण तटीय मैदान का उत्तरी भाग कोंकण तट के नाम से जाना जाता है। मध्य भाग को कनारा और दक्षिणी भाग को मालाबार तट का मालाबार क्षेत्र कहा जाता है।

questions.question: What is 'Golden Quadrilateral' related to?
questions.explanation: The Golden Quadrilateral is a national highway network connecting most of the major industrial, agricultural and cultural centers of India. It frames a quadrilateral connecting the four major cites of India i.e. Delhi, Kolkata, Mumbai, Chennai. Former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee made a formal announcement of the project in 1998.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an eligibility requirement to become the Vice President of India?
options: They should not hold any office of profit under the Central Government or any State Government or any local or other public authority.
He should have completed 40 years of age
He should be a citizen of India.
He should be qualified to be elected as a member of Rajya Sabha.
questions.explanation: The person who will elected for vice president past. He should have completed 40 years of age, this is not an eligibility requirement to become the vice precedent of India.

questions.question: Who became the acting Prime Minister of India after the death of Jawahar lal Nehru in 1964?
questions.explanation: Gulzari Lal Nanda was born in Siyalkote, Pakistan. He was the one who became two times the acting PM of India. 1st Tenure: - 27 May, 1964 – 9 June, 1964 [After demise of Pt. Nehru] 2nd Tenure: - 11 January, 1966 – 24 January, 1966 [After demise of Lal Bahadur Shashtri]

questions.question: Match the column A with column B and choose the correct option Column -A Column -B A) Protozoa 1) Roundworm B) Nematoda 2) Amoeba C) Arthopoda 3) Cockroach D) Echinodermata 4) Starfish
questions.explanation: Column-A Column-B Phylum Animal i. Protozoa - b. Amoeba ii. Nematoda - a. Roundworm iii.Arthropoda - c. Cockroach iv. Echinodermata - d. Starfish

questions.question: Antonie van Leeuwenhoek discovered single celled life forms in :
questions.explanation: Antonie Van Leeuwenhoek is credited with discovering single-celled organism, which he referred to as ''animalcules'' in 1674. Using his handcrafted microscopes, he was the first to observe and describe these microorganisms, laying the foundation for the field of microbiology

questions.question: Dadabhai Naoroji was the first person to discuss the concept of ............. in pre-independence India.
questions.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji in his book "Poverty and Un-British Rule in India" made earliest estimation of poverty line [Rs. 16 to Rs. 35 per capita year]. It was based on cost of subsistence or minimum basic diet [rice, flour, daal, mutton, ghee, salt, vegetable].

questions.question: Which of the following is a tertiary activity?
questions.explanation: Trading is a tertiary activity. Tertiary activities consist of all service occupations. Transport, communication, trade, health, education and administration are important examples of tertiary activities. It includes both production and exchange.

questions.question: Parsec is a unit of ______.?
questions.explanation: Quantity Unit: Acceleration= m/s2 Speed= km/h or m/sec. Time= second. Length= meter, km, light year, parsec etc. 1 light year = 9.46×10^15m 1 parsec = 3.26 light years

questions.question: Which one of the following features is not shown by metals?
options: Can be made into a wire
questions.explanation: Metals have the quality of reflecting light from its surface and can be polished. They are not dull but lustrous.

questions.question: In 1904 who discovered plum-pudding model of atom, according to which positive charge is spread over a sphere and electrons are embedded to the positive charge as resins are scattered in plum-pudding??
questions.explanation: The plum - pudding model of atom was postulated by J.J. Thomson in 1904. According this solid sphere and electrons are embedded in plosive charge sphere uniformly. This model is also calved water melon model of the atom.

questions.question: Recently, Operation Kamdhenu was launched by which institution?
questions.explanation: In March 2024, Jammu and Kashmir Police launched Operation Kamdhenu to curb cattle smuggling in Jammu, Samba and Kathua (JSK) police ranges.

questions.question: What is the name given to the brain chip released by Elon Musk’s company Neuralink? > एलन मस्क की कंपनी न्यूरालिंक द्वारा जारी ब्रेन चिप को क्या नाम दिया गया है?
questions.explanation: In January 2024, Elon Musk’s Neuralink company claimed to have implanted a brain chip in a human. It has been named ‘Telepathy’. > जनवरी 2024 में, एलोन मस्क की न्यूरालिंक कंपनी ने एक इंसान में ब्रेन चिप प्रत्यारोपित करने का दावा किया था। इसे 'टेलीपैथी' नाम दिया गया है

questions.question: Match the classical dance with the state and choose the correct option: Classical dance State A) Sattriya 1) Tamil Nadu B) Mohiniyattam 2) Kerla C) Kuchipudi 3) Assam D) Bharatnatyam 4) Andhra Pradesh
questions.explanation: Classical Dance State Bharatnatyam - Tamil Nadu Mohiniyattam - Kerala kuchipudi - Andhra Pradesh Sattriya - Assam

questions.question: Special traditional bath named ''Shahi Snan'' is a major event of which of the following festival? > शाही स्नान'' नामक विशेष पारंपरिक स्नान निम्नलिखित में से किस त्योहार का एक प्रमुख आयोजन है?
questions.explanation: Shahi Snan is a major festival of the Kumbh Mela. Kumbh Mela is held at four places Prayagraj,Haridwar, Nashik and Ujjain, Sadhaks believe that bathing in these rivers frees one from past mistakes and sins. Sahi Snan also known as Rajyoga Snan, the saints [sadhu] and his followers take a dip in the holy river at a fixed time. It is believed that taking bath in an auspicious time gives the blessing of immortality. > शाही स्नान कुंभ मेले का एक प्रमुख त्योहार है। कुंभ मेला चार स्थानों प्रयागराज, हरिद्वार, नासिक और उज्जैन में आयोजित किया जाता है, साधकों का मानना ​​है कि इन नदियों में स्नान करने से व्यक्ति को पिछली गलतियों और पापों से मुक्ति मिल जाती है। साही स्नान को राजयोग स्नान के रूप में भी जाना जाता है, संत [साधु] और उनके अनुयायी एक निश्चित समय पर पवित्र नदी में डुबकी लगाते हैं। मान्यता है कि शुभ मुहूर्त में स्नान करने से अमरत्व का वरदान मिलता है।

questions.question: The novel 'Midnight’s Children’ is written by...... > उपन्यास 'मिडनाइट्स चिल्ड्रेन’ किसके द्वारा लिखा गया है?
questions.explanation: Midnight’s Children is a novel written by Salman Rushdie in 1981. It portrays India’s transition from British Colonial rule to independence and the partition of India. It is considered an example of postcolonial postmodern and magical realist literature > मिडनाइट्स चिल्ड्रन 1981 में सलमान रुश्दी द्वारा लिखा गया एक उपन्यास है। यह ब्रिटिश औपनिवेशिक शासन से स्वतंत्रता और भारत के विभाजन तक भारत के संक्रमण को चित्रित करता है। इसे उत्तर औपनिवेशिक उत्तरआधुनिक और जादुई यथार्थवादी साहित्य का एक उदाहरण माना जाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following countries holds a permanent seat on the UN Security Council? > निम्नलिखित में से किस देश के पास संयुक्त राष्ट्र सुरक्षा परिषद में स्थायी सीट है?
questions.explanation: The United Nations Security Council [UNSC] is composed of 15 members. There are five permanent members China, France, Russian federations, the United States and the United Kingdom and 10 non-permanent members who are elected by the General Assembly. The non permanent members are elected for two years terms so every year the general assembly elects five non-permanent members out of total 10. > संयुक्त राष्ट्र सुरक्षा परिषद [यूएनएससी] 15 सदस्यों से बनी है। पांच स्थायी सदस्य चीन, फ्रांस, रूसी संघ, संयुक्त राज्य अमेरिका और यूनाइटेड किंगडम हैं और 10 गैर-स्थायी सदस्य हैं जो महासभा द्वारा चुने जाते हैं। गैर-स्थायी सदस्यों को दो साल के लिए चुना जाता है, इसलिए हर साल आम सभा कुल 10 में से पांच गैर-स्थायी सदस्यों का चुनाव करती है।

questions.question: Who is the winner of the Hindi Yuva Puruskar 2024? > हिंदी युवा पुरस्कार 2024 का विजेता कौन है?
questions.explanation: Gaurav Pandey won the Hindi Yuva Puraskar for his poetry collection Smritiyon Ke Beech Ghiri Hai Prithvi. > गौरव पांडे ने अपने काव्य संग्रह स्मृतियों के बीच घिरी है पृथ्वी के लिए हिंदी युवा पुरस्कार जीता।

questions.question: In which year was the coveted Dadasaheb Phalke Award instituted? > प्रतिष्ठित दादा साहब फाल्के पुरस्कार की शुरुआत किस वर्ष की गई थी?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke award was instituted in 1969. Dadasaheb Phalke is known as the 'Father of Indian Cinema'. He made first full length film of India titled Raja Harishchandra in 1913. The award comprises a Swarna Kamal Medallion, a shawl, and a cash prize on Rs. 10 Lakh. Devika Rani was the first recipient of Dada Saheb Phalke Award in 1969. It is presented by Directorate of Film Festivals under ministry of Information & Broadcasting > दादा साहब फाल्के पुरस्कार की स्थापना 1969 में की गई थी। दादा साहब फाल्के को 'भारतीय सिनेमा के जनक' के रूप में जाना जाता है। उन्होंने 1913 में राजा हरिश्चंद्र नामक भारत की पहली पूर्ण लंबाई वाली फिल्म बनाई। पुरस्कार में स्वर्ण कमल पदक, एक शॉल और 10 लाख रुपये की नकद राशि शामिल है। देविका रानी 1969 में दादा साहेब फाल्के पुरस्कार की पहली प्राप्तकर्ता थीं। यह सूचना और प्रसारण मंत्रालय के तहत फिल्म समारोह निदेशालय द्वारा प्रस्तुत किया जाता है।

questions.question: Which state government recently tested the country’s first rice ATM? > हाल ही में किस राज्य सरकार ने देश के पहले चावल एटीएम का परीक्षण किया?
questions.explanation: The country’s first Rice ATM has been inaugurated in Bhubaneswar, Odisha. 24-hour service will be available through this Rice ATM. > देश के पहले राइस एटीएम का उद्घाटन ओडिशा के भुवनेश्वर में किया गया है। इस राइस एटीएम के जरिए 24 घंटे सेवा मिलेगी

questions.question: Two equal circles pass through each other’s centres. If radius of each circle is 17√3 cm, the length of the common chord is?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704351763e3.22867499.png) AB=AC=BC= radius of circle AOD = common chord ∴AO = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704353d1170.02522834.gif) ×17√3 = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704355c0b11.90422632.gif) ∴AD = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704356e7078.49087623.gif) ×2 = 51cm

questions.question: If x is the least number which when divided by 21, 25 and 30 leaves remainder 2, 6 and 11 respectively and y is the HCF of the numbers 18, 54 and 81, find the value of x−y³?
The area of a parallelogram with base 32 cm and corresponding height 21 cm is:
questions.explanation: Base = 32, height = 21 Area = Base × height = 32×21 = 672 cm²

questions.question: Find the area of an equilateral triangle whose length of median is 5√3 cm.
questions.explanation: Let side of triangle be x. Length of median = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970435cd7435.63228783.gif) x = 5√3 x =10 Area = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970435de57a6.58711284.gif) ×10×10 = 25√3 cm²

questions.question: A dishonest shopkeeper professes to sell his goods at 10% loss on the cost price but uses a faulty weight that weighs 15% less. His profit/loss percentage [up to two decimal places] is:
questions.explanation: 10 % loss = 10 - 9 15 % less = 17 - 20 % gain = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970436740a64.84794833.gif) ×100 = 5.88%

questions.question: If the area of a square is 36x²+60x+25, then the perimeter of the square is:
questions.explanation: (36x²+60x+25 ⇒ (6x)²+(5)²+2(6x)(5) ⇒ (6x+5)² → Area Side = 6x+5 Perimeter = 4[6x+5] = 2[12x+10]

questions.question: The value of the expression: 221÷13×4−54×2÷18+676÷[{79 – 53}]
questions.explanation: 221÷13×4−54×2÷18+676÷[{79 – 53}] ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970436e721e4.68400834.gif) ×4- ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704370607c6.37057963.gif) +676÷[26] ⇒ 68-6+26 ⇒ 62+26 = 88

questions.question: The length and breadth of a rectangular piece of land is in the ratio 8:5. The owner spent Rs. 10,400 at the rate of Rs. 8 per metre for surrounding it from all sides. The difference between the length and breadth of the plot is?
questions.explanation: L:B = 8:5 Price Per metre = ₹ 8 Price = ₹10,400 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704374e9469.57871557.gif) = 1300 L:B = 8:5 26→1300 1→50 Length = 400, Breadth =250 Difference = 400-250 =150 m

questions.question: In a 5000m race, A gives B a start of 200 meter and wins the race by 30 seconds. If A gives B a start of 500 meter, the race ends in a dead heat. How long does B take to complete the race [in minutes]?
questions.explanation: A gives B head start of 500 m:- A=5000, B=4500 A:B = 10:9 Race ends in Dead heat:- A:B = 10x:9x → speed ratio A Gives B a start of 200 m:- ⇒ B-A = 30sec ⇒ ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704377d5214.04598756.gif) - ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970437951b62.35042580.gif) = 30 → ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970437a96a96.37751617.gif) = 30 x = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970437dc22a1.27386000.gif) Speed of B = 9× ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970437eb2419.26470653.gif) = 10 m/s Time of B = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043807aa23.62938900.svg) = 500 sec = 8 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043816d0c3.06818471.svg) mins

questions.question: The given bar graph shows the sales of Refrigerators from six branches [B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, and B6] of Godrej during two consecutive years 2005 and 2006. Study the bar graph carefully and answer the question that follows: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970438cc4133.38656203.png) What is the ratio of total sales of B1 and B2 for both years to the total sales of B5 and B6 for both the years?

In a chemistry lab, two bottles contain mixture of Acid and Water in the ratio of 3:4 in the first bottle and 9:5 in the second bottle. In what ratio the contents of the two bottles are mixed so that the new mixture contains water and acid in the ratio 2:3?
questions.explanation: Bottle 1 = 3:4 → A:W Bottle 2 = 9:5 → A:W Mixture = 3:2 → A:W 3:4 = 7 ×10 9:5 = 14 ×5 3:2 = 5 ×14 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970438f05d55.29645981.png) Taking Acid:- ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704391926d6.68840448.png) ⇒ 3:12 ⇒ 1:4 → Bottle 1: Bottle 2

questions.question: P and Q can do a certain work in 24 days and 36 days respectively. They work together for 6 days. R completes the remaining work in 21 days. P and R can complete ⅚ᵗʰ of the same work in how many days?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043956f521.78136443.png) P + Q in 6 days = 6×5 = 30 Remaining ⇒ 72-30 = 42 R completes in 21 days Efficiency of R = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043979c704.86640650.gif) = 2 P + R = 3 + 2 = 5 Time = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970439968461.94838079.gif) = 12 days

questions.question: Rs. 32,480 is divided between Ravi and Saurabh such that Ravi’s share after 9 years is equal to Saurabh’s share after 11 years at the 12.5% p.a. Compound Interest. Find the share of Ravi.
questions.explanation: Ravi's share in 9yrs = Saurabh's share in 11 years. 12.5% ⇒ 8 → 9 ⇒ 64 → 81 64:81 → S:R 64+81 = 145 → ₹32,480 1 → 224 Ravi share = 81 ×224 = ₹18,144

questions.question: Find the value of x, if x = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970439de11b9.89049692.gif) + ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970439ed71c1.25073625.gif)
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043a2f9499.10469698.png)

questions.question: A thief is spotted by a policeman from a distance of 500 metre. The thief was running at a speed of 18 km/hour and the policeman was chasing the thief at a speed of 28 km/hour. How much distance is covered by the policeman to catch the thief [in kms]?
questions.explanation: Relative distance =500 m Relative speed = 28-18 =10 km/hr = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043a601352.34525703.gif) m/s Time = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043a6effd7.52468798.gif) ⇒ 180 secs ⇒ 3 min Distance covered by Policeman:- ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043a80f023.53361828.gif) ×3 ⇒ 1.4 km

questions.question: One packet of biscuit costs Rs. 21 but four packets of biscuit costs Rs.72. What is the effective discount percentage?
questions.explanation: 1 packet = ₹ 21 4 packets = ₹84Rs. SP = ₹72 84:72 = 14:12 = 7:6 7→6 ∴ discount = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043adff1b1.18407314.gif) ×100 = 14.28%

questions.question: A and B entered into a partnership in the ratio ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043b283cd3.81572789.gif) : ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043b37bd88.20111679.gif) . A being the active partner got Rs. 4 lakhs out of the annual profit and remaining profit was divided between A and B in proportion to their investment. If A received a total profit of Rs. 9.25 lakh after one year, what is the annual profit?
questions.explanation: A:B = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043b7a0999.22458635.gif) : ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043b91a8d8.45007169.gif) )× 20 A:B = 5:2 A's share = 9.25-4 = 5.25 lakh 5 ⟶ 5.25 lakh 7 ⟶ 7.35 lakh Annual profit = 7.35+4 = 11.35 lakh

questions.question: A can complete a work in 27 days and B can complete the same work in 24 days. They start working together but B works for just 6 days. The remaining work is completed by A. If they receive Rs. 6400 after completion of the work, then what is A’s share in the work?
questions.explanation: B work = 24 Days B in 6 days = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043bd11b38.88033353.gif) = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043be06d75.32958667.gif) Remain ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043c06f155.51976660.gif) done by, A A:B = 3:1 4 → 6400 1 → 1600 A's share = 1600×3 = ₹4800

questions.question: The length of the shadow of a vertical tower on level ground increases by 24√3 metre when the altitude of the sun changes from 60° to 30°. Find the height of the tower.
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043c695d85.04523184.png) ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043c8c3621.93163584.png)

questions.question: The ratio of Boys to Girls in a college is 7:5. If 60 boys leave the college and 45 girls join the college then the ratio becomes 4: 5. Find the number of boys [final] in the college.
questions.explanation: Boys: Girls = 7x : 5x 60 boys leave & 45 girls join:- ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043ccdcd45.94789223.gif) = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043cdf40f3.14928145.gif) 35x-300 = 20x+180 15x = 480 x = 32 Final Boys:- 7×32-60 =164

questions.question: Train P takes 2 hours more than train Q to travel a distance of 960km. Due to engine trouble speed of Train Q falls by ¼ᵗʰ and it takes 2 hours more than Train P to complete the same journey. What is the original speed of Train Q [in km/hr]?
questions.explanation: speed: 4 :3 Time: 3:4 1----4hr 3----12hr 960/12 = 80km/h = speed of Q

questions.question: The following table shows India’s spending on different public expenditure in the year 2022. Study the table carefully and answer the question that follows: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043d455150.27330540.png) Expenditure on Defence was what percent of the total expenditure [correct up to two decimal places]?
questions.explanation: From the table: Defence = 975 Total = 742 +884+975+468 +1270+552 = 4891 %Defence = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043d69eb74.72140567.gif) ×100 = 19.93%

questions.question: In an election among three candidates P, Q and R. P got 25% more votes than Q whereas R got 16.66% more votes than Q. R also defeated Q by 7500 votes. If 82% of the voters voted and no invalid votes were there, what was the total number of voters in the voting list?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043d979dc8.32128282.png) P∶Q∶R = 15∶12∶14 → 41 41 → 82% 1 → 2% P : Q : R = 30% : 24% : 28% R-Q = 28-24 = 4% 4% → 7500 100% → 1,87,500 Total number of voters = 1,87,500

questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997043deac374.24014675.png)

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: Use FEWER PEOPLE. CONCEPT:- "Less" is used for uncountable nouns, while "fewer" is used for countable nouns. "People" is a countable noun, so "less people" is incorrect. The correct word should be "fewer." The correct sentence would be: "I deny the allegation that fewer people are buying our books."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Rashmika sat by the lake, gazing at the sunset with a _______expression, lost in deep thoughts about her future.
questions.explanation: The word "pensive" refers to a deep or serious thought, making it the most appropriate choice in this context. The sentence implies that Rashmika is lost in deep thoughts while gazing at the sunset, which aligns perfectly with the meaning of "pensive." Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Restive – बेचैन / अशांत – Unable to remain still; restless or impatient. Example: "The students grew restive during the long lecture." [Incorrect in this context, as Rashmika is thoughtful, not restless.] • Fervent – उत्साही / प्रबल – Showing intense passion or enthusiasm. Example: "He gave a fervent speech about human rights." [Incorrect in this context, as Rashmika is not showing intense passion but quiet contemplation.] • Pensive – विचारमग्न / चिंतनशील – Engaged in deep or serious thought. Example: "She sat alone, looking pensive as she pondered her future." [Correct, as it matches the idea of being lost in deep thoughts.] • Ordinary – साधारण / सामान्य – Not special or different; commonplace. Example: "It was just an ordinary evening at the park." [Incorrect, as the sentence suggests a reflective moment, not something ordinary.] Conclusion: Option [C] Pensive is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. He could not tell us why did she not eat her lunch.
questions.explanation: In indirect speech, the sentence structure should follow statement order [subject + verb] instead of question order [auxiliary verb + subject]. Option [C] "Why she had not eaten." is the most appropriate because the past perfect tense [had eaten] is preferred when referring to an action that occurred before another past action. The improved sentence: "He could not tell us why she had not eaten her lunch."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: You had better not to leave your belongings unattended; they might get stolen.
You had better not leave your belongings unattended; they would be stolen.
You had better not leave your belongings unattended; they might get stolen.
You had better not to leave your belongings unattended; they may get stolen.
questions.explanation: Correct Sentence: You had better not leave your belongings unattended; they might get stolen. Explanation: • The phrase "had better" is always followed by the bare infinitive [verb without "to"], so "not leave" is correct. • "Might" is appropriate because it expresses a possibility, which fits the warning context. • The semicolon [“;"] correctly separates two independent clauses.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: Twelfth is the correct spelling. • Twelfth – बारहवां – The number 12th in a sequence. Example: "She finished in the twelfth place in the competition." • Beggar [Correct] – भिखारी – A person who asks for money or food. Example: "The beggar sat near the temple, hoping for kindness." • Adamant [Correct] – दृढ़ – Refusing to be persuaded or to change one’s mind. Example: "She was adamant about her decision to study abroad." • Pursuit [Correct] – अनुसरण – The action of following or chasing something. Example: "His pursuit of knowledge never ended."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. While hiking in the mountains, you should look into for wild animals.
questions.explanation: Use "Look out." • The phrase "look into" means to investigate or examine something, which does not fit in this context. • The sentence requires a phrase that means to be careful or watch for danger, which is correctly expressed by "look out." Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Look into – जाँच करना- To investigate or examine. Example: "The police will look into the matter." • Look about – तलाश करना- To search or glance around. Example: "He looked about the room for his keys." • Look out – सावधान रहना, ध्यान देना- To be careful or watch for danger. Example: "Look out! There's a car coming." • Look after – देखभाल करना- To take care of something or someone. Example: "You should look after your pronunciation when you speak English."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - His triumph was overshadowed by an uneasy sense of foreboding.
questions.explanation: Sol: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Foreboding – पूर्वाभास / आशंका – A strong feeling that something bad is going to happen. Example: "She had a sense of foreboding before entering the abandoned house." • Spying – जासूसी – Secretly gathering information about someone or something. Example: "The detective was spying on the suspect." • Enthusiasm – उत्साह / जोश – A strong excitement or eagerness. Example: "He spoke about his new project with great enthusiasm." • Hatred – नफरत / घृणा – An intense dislike or hostility. Example: "His hatred for corruption made him a strong activist." • Apprehension – आशंका / चिंता – A feeling of anxiety or fear about the future. Example: "She felt apprehension before giving her speech."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. ABEYANCE
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Abeyance – निलंबन / स्थगन – A state of temporary disuse or suspension. Example: "The construction work was kept in abeyance due to legal issues." • Continuation – जारी रहना / विस्तार – The process of continuing something without stopping. Example: "The continuation of the project depended on government approval." • Shallow – उथला / सतही – Not deep in physical depth or thought. Example: "The river was shallow near the banks, making it easy to cross." • Suspension – निलंबन / अस्थायी रोक – The act of temporarily stopping something. Example: "The suspension of train services caused inconvenience to passengers." • Revelation – रहस्योद्घाटन / खुलासा – The act of revealing or making something known. Example: "His autobiography contained shocking revelations about his past."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. Let’s proceed ___________ the work in hand.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition to use with "proceed" in this context is "with." Correct Sentence: "Let’s proceed WITH the work in hand." • In – "In" is used to indicate location or position within something. Example: "She is in the room." [Incorrect in this context as it does not fit with 'proceed.'] • With – "With" is used to indicate association or involvement. Example: "Let’s proceed with the project." [Correct, as "proceed with" is the correct collocation when continuing a task.] • Of – "Of" is used to indicate possession or belonging. Example: "The pages of the book were torn." [Incorrect in this context as 'proceed' does not take 'of.'] • At – "At" is used to indicate a specific point or place. Example: "She is at the station." [Incorrect in this context as it does not fit with 'proceed.']

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Give somebody a ring
options: Gift someone a piece of jewellery
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Option [B] - Call someone on the telephone "Give somebody a ring" – "किसी को फोन करना" This idiom means to make a phone call to someone. It is an informal way of saying that you will contact someone by telephone. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase originates from the ringing sound made by telephones. Over time, it became a casual way of referring to calling someone. Example: "I’ll give you a ring once I reach home safely." Hindi: "मैं घर पहुँचते ही तुम्हें फोन करूँगा।"

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: No error. CONCEPT: The sentence is grammatically correct. • "For those" correctly introduces the subject referring to people. • "Who live by themselves" is a proper relative clause describing people who live alone. • "A smartphone provides a good company" is correctly structured, where "good company" refers to companionship. Since there are no grammatical errors, the correct choice is [D] No error.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. CONTRAVENE
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all words along with its usage: • Contravene – उल्लंघन करना / विरोध करना – To go against or violate a law, rule, or agreement. Example: "The company was fined for contravening environmental regulations." Now, let's analyze the given options: • Defend – बचाव करना – To protect or support someone or something. Example: "The lawyer defended his client in court." • Breach – उल्लंघन / तोड़ना – To break or violate a law, rule, or agreement. Example: "His actions led to a breach of contract." • Integrate – एकीकृत करना – To combine or bring together into a whole. Example: "The school integrated new technology into its curriculum." • Disagree – असहमत होना – To have a different opinion. Example: "She disagreed with his views on politics." Conclusion: "Breach" is the most appropriate synonym for "Contravene," as both words imply breaking or violating a rule or law.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- She slammed a mental door shut on the HARROWING thought.
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all words along with its usage: • Harrowing – यातनादायक / परेशान करने वाला – Extremely distressing, painful, or disturbing. Example: "The survivors shared their harrowing experiences of the earthquake." Now, let's analyze the given options: • Persistent – लगातार / स्थायी – Continuing for a long time or without stopping. Example: "His persistent efforts finally led to success." • Spontaneous – स्वतःस्फूर्त / अचानक – Happening naturally or without planning. Example: "Her spontaneous decision to travel surprised everyone." • Fierce – उग्र / भयंकर – Violently intense or aggressive. Example: "The fierce storm caused massive destruction." • Pleasant – सुखद / आनंददायक – Giving a sense of happiness or comfort. Example: "We had a pleasant evening by the beach." Conclusion: "Pleasant" is the most appropriate antonym for "Harrowing," as "harrowing" means distressing or painful, while "pleasant" means enjoyable or comforting.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. His strong _________ guided him to be a ____________ worker who always did the right thing.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: [B] Conscience, Conscientious. This sentence means that his moral sense [conscience] helped him become a responsible and dedicated [conscientious] worker who always acted ethically. Explanation of all words: • Conscious – "सचेत / जागरूक" – Being aware of one's surroundings or thoughts. Example: "She remained conscious after the fall." • Conscience – "अंतरात्मा / नैतिकता" – A person’s sense of right and wrong. Example: "His conscience didn’t allow him to lie." • Conscientious – "ईमानदार / कर्तव्यनिष्ठ" – Being careful, responsible, and dedicated to doing one's work properly. Example: "A conscientious student always completes assignments on time."

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A person who has recently started an activity
questions.explanation: Here are the explanations for all options: • Neologism – "नवशब्द" – A newly coined word or phrase. Example: "The word 'selfie' is a neologism that became popular in the 21st century." • Neolithic – "नवपाषाण युग से संबंधित" – Related to the later part of the Stone Age. Example: "Early humans in the Neolithic period developed agriculture." • Neophyte – "नवागंतुक / नवदीक्षित" – A person who is new to an activity, subject, or belief. Example: "As a neophyte in yoga, she was still learning the basic poses." • Dilettante – "शौक़ीन / ऊपरी ज्ञान रखने वाला" – A person who dabbles in an activity without serious commitment. Example: "He played the piano as a dilettante, without any real dedication." A neophyte is the most appropriate choice as it specifically means someone who has recently started an activity.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Despite harsh criticism, she's sticking to her guns on this issue.
options: Physically hold onto weapons for safety
To be indecisive and change opinions frequently
questions.explanation: Stick to One’s Guns – अपनी राय या निर्णय पर अड़े रहना "Stick to one's guns" means to refuse to change one's opinion or decision despite criticism or opposition. If someone "sticks to their guns," they remain firm in their beliefs, even when others try to persuade them otherwise. Origin of this Idiom: The phrase originates from military terminology, where soldiers were advised to stay at their artillery [guns] and keep fighting, even under enemy attack. Over time, it evolved into a metaphor for standing firm in one’s beliefs. Example: "Even after repeated warnings from his colleagues, John stuck to his guns and refused to change his stance on the project." [This means John did not change his opinion despite opposition.] Hindi Translation: "अपने सहयोगियों की बार-बार चेतावनी के बावजूद, जॉन अपने फैसले पर अड़ा रहा और अपनी राय नहीं बदली।"

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. A list of passengers and luggage
questions.explanation: Explanation: A waybill is a document that contains a list of passengers and luggage transported by a vehicle, ship, or aircraft. It serves as a record of the goods or people being transported and provides necessary details for tracking and verification. Here are the meanings and examples of the other options: • Waybill – " यात्रा दस्तावेज़" – A document listing details of passengers or goods being transported. Example: "The conductor checked the waybill to confirm the number of passengers on board." • Catalogue – "सूची " – A systematic list of items, often for sale or reference. Example: "She ordered a dress from the store's online catalogue." • Index – "अनुक्रमणिका" – A list of names, subjects, or topics with reference to their locations in a document or book. Example: "The book’s index helped me find the topic on renewable energy quickly." • Inventory – "माल सूची / भंडार" – A detailed list of goods or materials available in stock. Example: "The warehouse manager updated the inventory of electronic items."

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. He quickly picked it up and realized it was hungry and shivering. B. While walking home, Aryan noticed a tiny kitten lying helplessly near a tree. C. Over time, the kitten became healthy and playful, bringing joy to Aryan’s life. D. He took it home, gave it some warm milk, and wrapped it in a soft blanket.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: BADC This order makes sense because: • Sentence B introduces the situation, explaining that Aryan found a tiny kitten lying helplessly near a tree. • Sentence A follows naturally, describing his immediate reaction of picking up the kitten and realizing it needed help. • Sentence D continues the sequence, explaining how Aryan took care of the kitten by feeding and comforting it. • Sentence C concludes the paragraph by describing how the kitten eventually became healthy and brought happiness to Aryan’s life. Final paragraph: "While walking home, Aryan noticed a tiny kitten lying helplessly near a tree. He quickly picked it up and realized it was hungry and shivering. He took it home, gave it some warm milk, and wrapped it in a soft blanket. Over time, the kitten became healthy and playful, bringing joy to Aryan’s life."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The doctor advised me to give up sweets.
options: To me give up sweets was advised by doctor.
The doctor advised to give up sweets me.
I was advised by the doctor to give up sweets.
I was advised to give up eat sweets by the doctor.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: [C] I was advised by the doctor to give up sweets. Explanation: The given sentence "The doctor advised me to give up sweets." is in active voice, where the subject "The doctor" performs the action of advising. Active Voice Structure: • The doctor [Subject] + advised [Verb] + me [Object] + to give up sweets [Infinitive phrase]. Passive Voice Structure: • I [Object → Subject] + was advised [Passive Verb] + by the doctor [Agent] + to give up sweets [Infinitive phrase]. Thus, the correct transformation is: "I was advised by the doctor to give up sweets."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. She said, "What a beautiful dress it is!"
options: She said what a beautiful dress it was.
She exclaimed that it was a very beautiful dress.
She told that it was a very beautiful dress.
She exclaimed what a beautiful dress it was.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: [B] She exclaimed that it was a very beautiful dress. Explanation: In indirect speech, exclamatory sentences are changed into reported speech by using verbs like "exclaimed" to express emotions. • The direct speech, "What a beautiful dress it is!", is an exclamatory sentence. • The exclamation is replaced with "exclaimed that" to maintain the meaning and tone. • The verb "is" [present tense] changes to "was" [past tense] to follow the sequence of tenses rule. Thus, the correct transformation is: "She exclaimed that it was a very beautiful dress."

questions.question: Married life has a significant place in our Indian culture. It is the life of a householder that nurtures and enhances the society. The nation gets its future generation ____1____ to this. However, it is seen that there is more of bitterness and tension in the relationship owing to ____2____ quarrels and differences of opinion. Husband and wife get disconnected from each other. The children become orphaned emotionally and the family ____3____. For the couple to understand each other well, it is important that they are able to leave their fears and speak ____4____ their minds to each other. They should discuss freely; and even when they are upset with one another, it should be sorted out as soon as possible. There may be differences of opinion, but this should never lead to ____5____ of harmony in mind. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Owing to" is a prepositional phrase that means "because of" or "due to". In this sentence, it indicates that the nation gets its future generation as a result of married life and the family structure. Here's a brief explanation of the other options: Failing: means not succeeding or failing to achieve something, which doesn't fit the context of the sentence. Leading: means guiding or directing someone or something, which doesn't fit the context of the sentence. Falling: means dropping or coming down from a higher position, which doesn't fit the context of the sentence.

questions.question: Married life has a significant place in our Indian culture. It is the life of a householder that nurtures and enhances the society. The nation gets its future generation ____1____ to this. However, it is seen that there is more of bitterness and tension in the relationship owing to ____2____ quarrels and differences of opinion. Husband and wife get disconnected from each other. The children become orphaned emotionally and the family ____3____. For the couple to understand each other well, it is important that they are able to leave their fears and speak ____4____ their minds to each other. They should discuss freely; and even when they are upset with one another, it should be sorted out as soon as possible. There may be differences of opinion, but this should never lead to ____5____ of harmony in mind. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Incessant" means continuing or repeating without interruption or pause. In this sentence, it describes the quarrels and differences of opinion as being constant and unrelenting, which contributes to the bitterness and tension in the relationship. Here's a brief explanation of the other options: Pertinent: means relevant or related to the subject at hand. While pertinent quarrels might be relevant to the relationship, the word doesn't convey the same sense of constant or unrelenting conflict as "incessant". Suppliant: means asking or begging for something, often in a humble or submissive manner. This word doesn't fit the context of the sentence, as it describes a type of request or appeal rather than a type of conflict. Condiment: means a type of seasoning or spice used to add flavor to food. This word is completely unrelated to the context of the sentence and doesn't make sense in this context.

questions.question: Married life has a significant place in our Indian culture. It is the life of a householder that nurtures and enhances the society. The nation gets its future generation ____1____ to this. However, it is seen that there is more of bitterness and tension in the relationship owing to ____2____ quarrels and differences of opinion. Husband and wife get disconnected from each other. The children become orphaned emotionally and the family ____3____. For the couple to understand each other well, it is important that they are able to leave their fears and speak ____4____ their minds to each other. They should discuss freely; and even when they are upset with one another, it should be sorted out as soon as possible. There may be differences of opinion, but this should never lead to ____5____ of harmony in mind. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Disintegrates" means to break or fall apart into smaller pieces, often in a state of decay or destruction. In this sentence, it describes the family as breaking apart or falling apart, often due to the emotional distance and conflict between the parents. Here's a brief explanation of the other options: Rejuvenates: means to make someone or something feel or look younger and more energetic. This word is the opposite of what is intended in the sentence, as the family is breaking apart rather than being revitalized. Execrates: means to strongly condemn or curse something. While this word implies a strong negative emotion, it doesn't quite fit the context of the sentence, which describes the family as breaking apart rather than being condemned. Emancipates: means to set someone free from oppression or control. This word is also not suitable for the context of the sentence, as the family is breaking apart rather than being freed.

questions.question: Married life has a significant place in our Indian culture. It is the life of a householder that nurtures and enhances the society. The nation gets its future generation ____1____ to this. However, it is seen that there is more of bitterness and tension in the relationship owing to ____2____ quarrels and differences of opinion. Husband and wife get disconnected from each other. The children become orphaned emotionally and the family ____3____. For the couple to understand each other well, it is important that they are able to leave their fears and speak ____4____ their minds to each other. They should discuss freely; and even when they are upset with one another, it should be sorted out as soon as possible. There may be differences of opinion, but this should never lead to ____5____ of harmony in mind. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Speak out" is an idiomatic expression that means to express one's thoughts or opinions openly and honestly. In this sentence, it emphasizes the importance of couples communicating openly and freely with each other, without holding back their thoughts or feelings. Here's a brief explanation of the other options: Up: doesn't fit the context of the sentence. "Speak up" is a different idiomatic expression that means to speak loudly or to express one's opinion boldly, but it's not the best fit for this sentence. Over: doesn't fit the context of the sentence. "Speak over" someone means to interrupt or talk over them, which is not the intended meaning. Through: doesn't fit the context of the sentence. "Speak through" something means to communicate indirectly or through a medium, which is not the intended meaning.

questions.question: Married life has a significant place in our Indian culture. It is the life of a householder that nurtures and enhances the society. The nation gets its future generation ____1____ to this. However, it is seen that there is more of bitterness and tension in the relationship owing to ____2____ quarrels and differences of opinion. Husband and wife get disconnected from each other. The children become orphaned emotionally and the family ____3____. For the couple to understand each other well, it is important that they are able to leave their fears and speak ____4____ their minds to each other. They should discuss freely; and even when they are upset with one another, it should be sorted out as soon as possible. There may be differences of opinion, but this should never lead to ____5____ of harmony in mind. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Disruption" means a disturbance or interruption that causes something to stop functioning or to become unstable. In this sentence, it refers to the disruption of harmony in mind, implying that differences of opinion should not cause a breakdown in mental peace and stability. Here's a brief explanation of the other options: Abduction: means the act of taking someone away against their will, often in a violent or forceful manner. This word is unrelated to the context of the sentence. Absorption: means the process of taking in or assimilating something, such as ideas, information, or substances. While absorption can be a mental process, it doesn't fit the context of the sentence. Degeneration: means the process of deteriorating or declining in quality, often due to disease, injury, or aging. While degeneration can be a mental or emotional process, it doesn't fit the context of the sentence.
